title: psittacanthus obovatus
intro: Psittacanthus obovatus, also known as the "monkey brush," is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It is a type of mistletoe, a hemiparasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of another plant and drawing water and nutrients through its haustoria. The leaves and stems of Psittacanthus obovatus are used in traditional medicine, said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and are believed to offer health benefits including relief from respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Psittacanthus obovatus are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
